repair_candidate.summary: Repair #92654 in place so the Dashboard/Control UI webchat reply feature can be reviewed cleanly. Keep the PR focused on right-click Reply, preserve @programmingWTF's contributor branch, and address all hydrated Copilot/ClawSweeper findings before any merge preflight.
repair_candidate.pr_title: fix(control-ui): repair Dashboard webchat reply affordance
repair_candidate.pr_body:
## What Problem This Solves

Repairs the open Dashboard/Control UI webchat Reply implementation from #92654 so users can right-click a message, choose Reply, see a composer preview, and send a quoted reply without regressing native context menus, keyboard dismissal, accessibility, or message formatting.

## Why This Change Was Made

The feature is the right canonical path for #16896, but hydrated Copilot and ClawSweeper comments still block merge. The repair should keep the contributor branch narrow and address the review findings: only suppress the native context menu when a custom reply action is available, remove the one-shot Escape listener bug, avoid Date.now-derived reply message IDs, build the custom menu without innerHTML, add menu/focus semantics, escape/normalize sender labels before Markdown quote insertion, clear chatReplyTarget only after send is accepted, guard delayed listener registration, and revert unrelated CSS focus/font-size churn.

## User Impact

Users get a focused right-click Reply workflow in Dashboard webchat without losing ordinary right-click behavior when the feature is unavailable and without accidental reply-target loss on aborted sends.

## Evidence

Planned validation: `pnpm test ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts` and `pnpm check:changed`. Source PR: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/92654. Canonical issue: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/16896. Prior related attempt: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/86679.
credit_notes: Repair the existing #92654 contributor branch instead of replacing it; preserve @programmingWTF as PR author/source.
Credit prior closed attempt #86679 by @mvanhorn in PR body context if reusing any implementation detail from that attempt.
Release-note context belongs in the PR body or squash message; do not edit CHANGELOG.md for this normal PR.
